name: Outlines (Structured LLM Output via Constrained Decoding)

auth.notes: Local inference; API providers use their own auth

performance.notes: Constrained decoding adds ~10-20% overhead but guarantees valid output

agent_readiness.notes: Guarantees structured output via constrained decoding (not post-processing). Works with local models (transformers, vLLM, llamacpp, mlx) and some APIs. @outlines.prompt template decorator. outlines.generate.json(model, Pydantic_schema). outlines.generate.regex(model, pattern). outlines.generate.choice(model, ['a', 'b']). Grammar-constrained generation. Unlike instructor (which retries on parse failure), Outlines generates valid output on first try. Best for local models needing guaranteed structure.

gotchas: Works best with local models (constrained decoding needs token access)
API provider support limited vs instructor
Complex Pydantic schemas may slow generation
best_when: Local model inference needing guaranteed valid structured output
Extracting structured data from text with high reliability
vLLM or transformers deployments needing JSON/regex constrained output
avoid_when: API-only LLMs (OpenAI, Anthropic) — instructor works better
Simple extraction where instructor's retry approach is sufficient
Teams unfamiliar with constrained decoding concepts
summary: Outlines guarantees structured LLM output via constrained decoding — the model is mathematically constrained to produce valid JSON/regex matches on the first try. Works with local models (transformers, vLLM, llamacpp, mlx). generate.json(model, PydanticSchema), generate.regex(model, pattern). Unlike instructor (retry-based), Outlines guarantees validity. Best for local model deployments where guaranteed-valid structured output without retries is critical.
